summary_markdown:
## What this issue is about
This issue discusses adding a linter called `rstcheck` to our project. `rstcheck` helps validate reStructuredText (RST) format, which is commonly used for documentation in Python packages. 

## What needs to be done
We need to integrate `rstcheck` into our project to enhance documentation quality. There’s also a suggestion to consider using `twine check`, which focuses more narrowly on the README file for PyPI. The preference so far seems to lean towards implementing `rstcheck` due to its broader coverage.

## Helpful background
`rstcheck` is a tool found [here](https://pypi.org/project/rstcheck/) that checks RST syntax for errors. While `twine check` validates the README for PyPI, it doesn’t check other RST files, making `rstcheck` a more comprehensive choice for our project. Review the [README-renderer documentation](https://pypi.org/project/readme-renderer/) for more context on `twine check`.

## Good first steps
1. Familiarize yourself with `rstcheck` and how it works by reviewing its documentation.
2. Examine the suggested integration method from the comments.
3. Consider testing the linter with some existing documentation files to see what issues might arise.
4. If you feel comfortable, you can start implementing `rstcheck` in our project and create a pull request for review.
